title: Lean Multi-Provider Debate Framework: Cross-Provider Adversarial Discourse for Calibrated AI Answers
description:
Lean Multi-Provider Debate is a multi-agent debate engine that pits two heterogeneous AI providers (Google Gemini and Anthropic Claude) against each other to produce calibrated, adversarially-vetted answers. Cross-provider debate prevents same-model echo chambers; Delphi iterative refinement and argument-graph construction structure the discourse; probabilistic calibration tracking measures answer quality over rounds.

Empirical sycophancy research: A two-dimensional model demonstrates that echo-chamber effects and false-consensus dynamics are independent failure modes that require different interventions. Same-provider debates exhibit higher sycophancy than cross-provider debates, even when prompts are identical.

Features: Cross-provider debate, Delphi iterative refinement, argument graph construction, probabilistic calibration tracking, post-debate interactive chat, semantic similarity analysis between debate turns.

Open source under Apache 2.0.
notes: Single-author research artifact. Cross-provider design (Google Gemini + Anthropic Claude) is the central design choice; same-provider debate is empirically shown to produce higher sycophancy.
